context.docs: CLAUDE.md (living source of truth — overrides the spec on conflict) + CDP-BUILD-SPEC.md §9B (automation workflow engine), §8 (rule-AST→SQL compiler, reused for branch conditions), §9 (outbox→Dispatcher pipeline)
env: Monorepo: pnpm workspaces + Turborepo + Vitest + strict TS. Run from repo root. Node 20+.

Real Postgres (dev): DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp (migrations applied). Single test file: pnpm --filter <pkg> exec vitest run path. Whole suite: DATABASE_URL=… pnpm test. Typecheck: pnpm typecheck. Lint: pnpm lint.

Browser e2e: pnpm --filter @cdp/web test:e2e — its OWN DB cdp_e2e on its OWN ports (local-api :8788, web :5174), runs with LOCAL_SES_FORCE_MOCK=1 LOCAL_SES_DKIM_STATUS=SUCCESS; re-seeds web/e2e/seed.ts each run. The dev stack (cdp, :8787/:5173) coexists. Playwright config has retries:1.
Dev API for manual checks: pnpm --filter @cdp/service-local-api dev:api (background, restart after backend edits). Health: GET http://localhost:8787/health.

Key reuse points: services/automation-runner (dsl.ts, core.ts, run.ts, enroll.ts), services/local-api (handlers.ts, routes.ts, app.ts, server.ts), services/dispatcher (core.ts, dispatch.ts), packages/segments (AST + compiler + rule builder), packages/email, web/src/screens (BroadcastComposer email-instance flow, TemplateEditor, SegmentBuilder), web/src/ui/kit.tsx (ActionMenu, Drawer, Button, Field, Select), web/e2e/seed.ts.

pnpm build (24/24 successful)
implReport.e2eSummary: No e2e — backend-only phase (phase.frontend=false; no builder UI, phases 5/6).
implReport.notes: PART A (send-node instance): POST /automations/:id/send-nodes/:nodeId/attach-template clones a library template into a kind='copy' working copy (source_template_id; copies design + subject/sender_id/to_address/from_selected via the same INSERT...SELECT as cloneTemplate) and repoints the send node's template_id at the copy; source SELECT scoped to ctx.workspaceId (cross-workspace 404, inv.2). POST /automations/:id/activate adds publish gating that did not exist before: pure collectSendNodeEnvelopeGaps (automation-runner dsl.ts, BFS order) feeds sendBroadcast-ordered per-node 409s (sender_id -> to_address -> subject, naming the node) then the verified sending_domains gate (only when >=1 send node; a no-send automation activates ungated). Dispatcher UNCHANGED — a automation outbox row's template_id is the copy, so dispatch.ts resolves sender_id->From + renders subject/To/body exactly as for broadcasts (verified end-to-end via the integration test: messages_log.automation_id set, broadcast_id null, exactly-once on replay). PART B (event-sourced set_attribute): new @cdp/shared event.ts (event.* namespace twin of customer.ts) + expression.ts (ValueSpec, renderExpression shared {{token}} engine, resolveValueSpec with literal | expression | legacy-bare-scalar + safe-empty). validateAutomationDefinition extended with validateSetAttributeValue (pure). buildEnrollmentInsertWithState persists {event:{type,payload,event_id}} onto automation_enrollments.state ONLY in enrollFromEvent; the other three enrollment paths keep the plain insert so state.event is undefined and an event.* expression resolves safe-empty. run.ts loads the profile + state.event.payload under the FOR UPDATE lock and resolves the value spec before the existing parameterized buildSetAttribute (workspace_id at $1) — read-only substitution, never SQL (inv.6 untouched); idempotent because the source state is immutable. NO migration (automation_enrollments.state jsonb DEFAULT '{}' already exists, migration 0005). NO builder UI (phases 5/6). Not pushed.
implReport.testSummary: All affected backend suites GREEN on a cold run: @cdp/shared 55/55 (4 files), @cdp/service-dispatcher 71/71 (12 files), @cdp/service-automation-runner 165/165 (31 files), @cdp/service-local-api 189/189 (43 files). New phase-4 tests: expression-value (20 unit, value resolver literal/customer.*/event.*/deep-dot/undefined-safe), set-attribute-value-spec (10 unit, validateSetAttributeValue + side-effect carries the spec), enrollment-event-state (5 unit, buildEnrollmentInsertWithState + intent threads the event), set-attribute-event-sourced.integration (2, real Postgres: state.event persisted, literal + {{event.amount}} written, undefined path safe-empty, workspace-scoped, idempotent retry), automation-send-dispatcher-instance.integration (1, runner->real dispatcher: From from sender_id, subject/To/body merge rendered, messages_log automation_id, exactly-once), automation-send-node-clone.integration (3, clone kind=copy + envelope copy + cross-ws refusal), automation-publish-gate.integration (7, ordered per-node 409s + verified-domain gate + no-send ungated + cross-ws 404). Updated parse-event-trigger.test.ts for the new intent.event field. No mock of Postgres in the integration tier; SES mocked via an injected SesEmailClient.

invariants: Tenant isolation: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL and every query filters by workspace_id. The automation-runner + enrollment + handlers connect with the Supabase SERVICE ROLE which BYPASSES RLS — they MUST scope by workspace_id IN CODE (RLS is not their safety net). workspace_id is loaded FROM the enrollment/automation row, never assumed.

workspace_id is NEVER client-supplied — it comes from the authorizer-injected claim (admin API) or a trusted server-set field (the enrollment row). Never trust a workspace_id in a request body. Reject a cross-workspace template_id / segment_id / sender_id (inv.2) — like validateSenderId does today.

All automation action SENDS go through the SAME outbox → Dispatcher pipeline as broadcasts (§9): suppression → frequency-cap → quiet-hours, gated on a VERIFIED sending domain. A send node carries an email INSTANCE whose envelope (real named From sender + To + Subject) is ALL mandatory — NO no-reply fallback (mirror sendBroadcast gating). The Dispatcher renders merge tags in the SUBJECT, To AND body.

Runner idempotency + concurrency (unchanged guarantee): one tick advances a due enrollment exactly once (SELECT … FOR UPDATE row lock in one tx, re-check active inside the lock; legacy CAS path for unit tests). Outbox dedupe_key per (automation_id, profile_id, node_id) prevents double-sends on retry. MAX_STEPS_PER_TICK guards pathological graphs. New node execution must preserve all of this.

Webhook action safety: outbound HTTP ONLY to an explicit per-workspace domain ALLOWLIST; enforce a timeout + BOUNDED retries; block SSRF (no internal/link-local/loopback/169.254 metadata targets); secrets (e.g. auth headers) use the @cdp/db secret-crypto envelope pattern, never returned in plaintext over the API; a webhook failure is ISOLATED (recorded; never crashes the tick or double-advances). LOCAL: the HTTP call is mocked/injected — never hit a real external host in tests.

Workspace timezone governs ALL automation time math (waits, wait-until, hour-of-day window) — DST-correct (reuse the zoned↔UTC approach from the broadcast scheduler: zonedInputToUtcIso/tzOffsetMs). One configurable timezone per workspace; never per-broadcast guesswork.

The DSL ({startNode, nodes}) is the SINGLE graph model; validateAutomationDefinition is the structural gate (exactly ONE trigger, every edge resolves, a reachable exit, and — extended — NO cycles / NO orphan nodes / down-only). The builder is an AUTO-LAYOUT RENDER of that graph: compute (x,y) from the tree, draw rounded orthogonal connectors, branches fan sideways. NO separate graph model, NO stored coordinates, NO loops/back-edges.

REUSE, do not reinvent (code-quality bar): the broadcast email-INSTANCE/clone flow (kind=copy, source_template_id, cloneTemplate, the From/To/Subject envelope + editor return flow) for send nodes; the segment rule builder + AstNode for the if/condition node; the kit ActionMenu / Drawer / Button (auto-locks on promise-returning onClick) / Field / Select; the Dispatcher core; scopedQuery; runInWorkspaceTx; evaluateRealtimeSegmentsForProfile. Match the surrounding code style, comment density and naming.

UI standing rules: NEVER use native confirm/alert/prompt — use ui/dialog.tsx (askConfirm) or purpose-built modals. EVERY server-calling button shows a spinner + locks until the response (kit Button auto-locks when onClick RETURNS the promise — wire it that way). The email editor autosaves (no manual save). PRESERVE every existing data-testid; add stable testids for new UI. Floating toasts (ui/toast.tsx showToast) for transient feedback.

Test-first (§16A/§18): write the failing acceptance tests FIRST, then implement to green. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier — use the REAL local DB. Mock SES/AWS (aws-sdk-client-mock / LocalStack); the webhook HTTP client is injected/mocked. Playwright (real browser) for UI phases with seeded data + stable selectors. turbo test task is cache:false — keep it. Unique workspace UUIDs + file-local namespaces (events.event_id / ses_message_id are GLOBAL keys).

Process hygiene: BUMP the root package.json version per change (patch=tiny, minor=feature, major only when told). UPDATE CLAUDE.md when behavior/architecture changes. Apply new SQL migrations to BOTH the dev DB (cdp) AND the e2e DB (cdp_e2e) via a node pg script (psql is unavailable). RESTART the dev API (pnpm --filter @cdp/service-local-api dev:api — a long-lived tsx process, NOT watch) after backend edits, and REBUILD a changed package (tsc -b) the local-api imports from dist. Scope discipline: build EXACTLY the agreed design — no extra features.
context.loop: Backend phase: pure unit tests + DB-integration against REAL local Postgres (DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp). Mock AWS/SES (aws-sdk-client-mock) / LocalStack; the webhook HTTP client is injected/mocked. No frontend.

criteria: A send node clones its template into an independently-editable copy (kind=copy); editing the copy does not touch the library original; the envelope persists on the copy. Cross-workspace template/sender ids are refused (inv.2).

Activating a automation with a send node missing From/To/Subject is refused with a clear, node-specific message; once all are set (real named sender, no no-reply fallback) + a verified domain exists, it activates. Verified against REAL Postgres.
A automation send flows through outbox → Dispatcher → messages_log(automation_id) with subject/To/body merge tags rendered; passes suppression/cap/quiet-hours. AWS/SES mocked.

An update-profile (set_attribute) step writes the profile attribute with a LITERAL value AND with an event-sourced value (e.g. attributes.last_purchase_amount = {{event.amount}} taken from the trigger event persisted on enrollment.state); the write is workspace-scoped + idempotent on retry; an undefined path resolves safely. Verified against REAL Postgres with unit tests for the value resolver.
phase.refs: CLAUDE.md (broadcast email-INSTANCE/clone model, sendBroadcast gating, dispatcher automation path, customer.* merge) + §9B action(send|set_attribute)
scope: Give each SEND node its OWN editable email instance, reusing the broadcast flow: attaching a template CLONES it (kind=copy, source_template_id) into the node's working copy; the envelope (real named From sender + To token + Subject) lives on that copy and is edited in the email editor (autosaved). The send node references the copy template_id.

PUBLISH-time gating: a automation cannot be activated if any send node lacks a sendable email (From sender + To + Subject all set) — mirror sendBroadcast's ordered 409s + the verified-domain gate. Surface which node/what is missing.

Verify the Dispatcher automation path end-to-end: automation outbox row → Dispatcher resolves sender_id → renders subject/To/body merge tags (the v0.27.2 subject fix) → messages_log with automation_id. No change to the send pipeline beyond what the instance model needs.

UPDATE-PROFILE action (the set_attribute action kind already exists + the runner already applies a STATIC value via buildSetAttribute): extend it so the value can be a LITERAL **or** an EXPRESSION sourced from the trigger event or the profile — e.g. customer.* and a new event.* namespace ({{event.<payload path>}}). To enable event.*, PERSIST the trigger event payload onto automation_enrollments.state at enrollment time (event-trigger enrollment from phase 3) so a later update-profile step can read it; resolve the expression at execution against the profile + enrollment.state.event (reuse the @cdp/shared customer.* resolver; add an analogous event.* resolver). A missing/again-undefined path resolves safely (no crash; documented — skip or empty).

Extend validateAutomationDefinition for the set_attribute value spec (literal vs expression) and keep it pure + unit-tested. The runner write stays workspace-scoped + idempotent (re-applying the same set_attribute on a retried tick is naturally idempotent for a literal; for an event-sourced value it resolves from the persisted enrollment.state, so a retry yields the same write).
phase.title: Action nodes: send-email instance + update-profile (event-sourced)

implUnits: 1. @cdp/shared: add the event.* namespace resolver — EVENT_PREFIX, resolveEventPath, eventMerge, expandEventToken — modeled on customer.ts; export from the package index. (No DB; pure.)

2. @cdp/shared: extract renderExpression (the {{token}} interpolation) so the dispatcher's renderTemplateBody and the new value resolver share one substitution engine; define resolveValueSpec(spec, ctx) with literal | expression | legacy-bare-scalar handling and safe-empty undefined resolution.

3. automation-runner dsl.ts: define the ValueSpec type ({kind:'literal', value} | {kind:'expression', expression}); extend ActionNode.value typing; add validateSetAttributeValue and call it from validateNodeFields' set_attribute branch (accept legacy bare scalar for back-compat).

4. automation-runner core.ts: change the set_attribute SideEffect to carry the VALUE SPEC (not a pre-resolved value); update actionSideEffect accordingly. Keep buildSetAttribute unchanged (it receives the already-resolved value).
5. automation-runner core.ts: add buildEnrollmentInsertWithState (persists state jsonb) alongside buildEnrollmentInsert; thread the trigger event payload through the event-enrollment intent.

6. automation-runner enroll.ts: in enrollFromEvent, persist {event:{type,payload,event_id}} onto enrollment.state via buildEnrollmentInsertWithState; segment_entry/manual paths keep buildEnrollmentInsert (state stays default '{}').

7. automation-runner run.ts: in the FOR-UPDATE tick load enrollment.state (already part of the row read — add state to the SELECT) and the profile; when applying a set_attribute side effect, call resolveValueSpec({profile, event: state.event}) THEN buildSetAttribute(ws, profile, key, resolvedValue). Resolution is read-only against the locked row + post-loaded profile, so a retry re-resolves identically (idempotent).

8. local-api handlers.ts: add a automation send-node CLONE path (reuse cloneTemplate's INSERT...SELECT copying design+envelope columns; kind='copy', source_template_id) and point the send node's template_id at the copy; scope the source SELECT by ctx.workspaceId (cross-workspace refusal).

9. local-api handlers.ts: add publish-time gating to the activate path (updateAutomation setting status='active', or a dedicated POST /automations/:id/activate) — for each send node, load its copy's (sender_id,to_address,subject) and emit sendBroadcast-ordered 409s naming the node; then the verified-domain gate. Reuse validateSenderId for any sender writes.
10. local-api routes.ts: register any new route(s) under the manage_content capability (clone/attach) consistent with existing automation routes; activation stays manage_content.

11. Process hygiene: bump root package.json (minor — feature); update CLAUDE.md (event.* namespace, set_attribute value spec, enrollment.state.event persistence, automation send-node instance + publish gate); NO new migration (automation_enrollments.state already exists). Rebuild changed packages (tsc -b) before restarting dev:api; apply nothing to the DB schema.
mocks: SES: an injected SesEmailClient capturing SendEmailInput (aws-sdk-client-mock style / a hand fake) — NEVER sends real mail; assert From/To/Subject/body and ConfigurationSet.
SQS: the injected SqsSender (SendMessageCommand) is a fake capturing enqueued {outbox_id} bodies (the runner boundary).
Clock: deps.now() injected for deterministic cap/quiet-hours/wait math.
No Postgres mock in the integration tier — REAL local Postgres (DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp); pure tiers use in-memory objects only.
Webhook HTTP client: not exercised this phase (no webhook nodes), but unchanged injection contract preserved.
plan.notes: automation_enrollments.state jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}' ALREADY EXISTS (packages/db/supabase/migrations/0005_messaging.sql) — CONFIRMED, NO migration needed to persist the trigger event there. Today buildEnrollmentInsert (core.ts) does NOT write state, so all current enrollment paths leave it at the default '{}'. The trigger event is in hand ONLY in enrollFromEvent (phase 3, EventRow with payload/type/event_id); that is the single place to persist state.event (via a new buildEnrollmentInsertWithState). The OTHER enrollment paths (enrollFromSegmentChange, enrollProfileManually, enrollSegmentSnapshot) have NO event, so they keep the plain buildEnrollmentInsert and state.event stays UNDEFINED — therefore a set_attribute with an event.* expression on a segment/manual-enrolled profile MUST resolve safely to empty (no crash): this is a required, explicitly-tested behavior, not an edge case. Activation today is just updateAutomation setting status='active' (no publish gating exists yet); the new gate mirrors sendBroadcast's ordered 409s (sender_id -> to_address -> subject) PER SEND NODE, then the verified-domain gate, naming the offending node. The dispatcher already renders subject/To/body merge tags and resolves sender_id->From with NO no-reply fallback reachable for a configured instance, so the automation send path needs no dispatcher change beyond the instance model the broadcast flow established. Idempotency for the event-sourced write is structural: the value re-resolves from the IMMUTABLE persisted enrollment.state under the FOR UPDATE lock, so a retried tick produces the identical jsonb_set write (a literal is trivially idempotent; an expression is idempotent because its source — state.event — never changes). All resolution is read-only string substitution (never interpolated into SQL), so invariant 6 is untouched; the only SQL is the existing parameterized buildSetAttribute with workspace_id at $1.
pureFunctions: @cdp/shared resolveEventPath(payload, pathAfterEvent): deep-dot resolver into the event payload (analogous to expandCustomerPath) — returns the leaf value or undefined.

@cdp/shared eventMerge(event): build the merge map for the 'event.*' namespace from a persisted trigger event payload (FULL tokens 'event.<path>' -> stringified scalars; nested objects/arrays skipped for whole-tag substitution, but dotted leaf paths supported via resolveEventPath). Pairs with the renderer like customerMerge.
@cdp/shared expandEventToken(token) / EVENT_PREFIX: normalize an 'event.*' token (no shorthand to expand, but keep symmetry with expandCustomerToken).

resolveValueSpec(spec, ctx:{profile, event}): the value resolver — a literal returns spec.value (or a bare scalar for back-compat); an expression renders via a shared interpolation over {customer.*, event.*} merge maps; an undefined/unknown path resolves to a documented safe-empty value; never throws. Lives in @cdp/shared (used by the runner; symmetrical with renderTemplateBody).

renderExpression(template, merge): the shared {{token}} interpolation engine extracted from dispatcher renderTemplateBody so the value resolver and the dispatcher share ONE substitution rule (unknown tags safe-empty for value resolution; same expandCustomerToken normalization).
isExpressionSpec(value) / isLiteralSpec(value) / coerceLegacyLiteral(value): pure value-spec discriminators used by both validateAutomationDefinition and resolveValueSpec.
validateSetAttributeValue(id, value): pure structural validation of the set_attribute value spec (literal vs expression vs legacy bare scalar), invoked from validateAutomationDefinition.

buildEnrollmentInsertWithState(workspaceId, automationId, profileId, startNode, state): SqlStatement that persists automation_enrollments.state (e.g. {event:{...}}) on insert; ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING preserved; workspace_id at $1; THROWS on falsy workspaceId.
buildSetAttribute (existing, core.ts) reused verbatim once the value is resolved — it already does workspace-scoped jsonb_set; resolution happens BEFORE building this statement.

collectSendNodeEnvelopeGaps(definition, copiesById): pure helper that walks send nodes and yields per-node {nodeId, missing:['sender'|'to'|'subject']} in sendBroadcast order — feeds the publish-gate message.
reuse: @cdp/shared customer.ts (customerMerge, expandCustomerToken, expandCustomerPath, resolveCustomerField) — the event.* resolver is a structural twin; renderExpression is extracted from dispatcher renderTemplateBody so both share ONE {{token}} engine.

dispatcher core.ts renderTemplateBody / buildSendEmailInput — already render subject/To/body merge tags (the v0.27.2 subject fix); the automation send path needs NO dispatcher change beyond the instance model already supporting sender_id/to_address/subject on the template.
automation-runner core.ts buildAutomationOutboxInsert / buildAutomationDedupeKey / buildSetAttribute / buildEnrollmentInsert — reuse verbatim; add buildEnrollmentInsertWithState as a sibling.

automation-runner run.ts chainTick + runEnrollmentInTx FOR-UPDATE lock + advanceFor — the set_attribute resolution slots into the existing side-effect application loop; idempotency/concurrency guarantees are preserved unchanged.

automation-runner enroll.ts enrollFromEvent (phase 3) — the only enrollment path that has the trigger event in hand; it is where state.event is persisted. enrollFromSegmentChange / enrollProfileManually / enrollSegmentSnapshot intentionally leave event undefined.

local-api handlers.ts cloneTemplate (INSERT...SELECT copying design + subject/sender_id/to_address/from_selected), validateSenderId (cross-workspace sender refusal, inv.2), sendBroadcast's ordered 409 messages + verified-domain gate — mirror these verbatim for the automation send-node clone + publish gate.

Existing test scaffolding: action-send-through-dispatcher.integration.test.ts (runner->real dispatcher with mocked SES, verified/unverified/suppressed cases), packages/shared/test/customer.test.ts (resolver unit-test style), services/local-api/test/broadcast-send-gate.integration.test.ts (ordered 409 gate assertions), automation-enroll-endpoint.integration.test.ts (enrollment scoping).

@cdp/segments AstNode + evaluateEventPayloadFilter (already in core.ts) — the event payload is already trusted/closed-grammar at enroll time; the value resolver reads the SAME persisted payload, no new whitelist needed for resolution (it's read-only substitution, never SQL).

assertions: resolveValueSpec({kind:'literal', value:42}, ctx) returns 42 unchanged (number preserved, not stringified).
resolveValueSpec({kind:'literal', value:'gold'}, ctx) returns 'gold'.
resolveValueSpec({kind:'literal', value:null}, ctx) returns null (an explicit null literal is honored, distinct from undefined).

resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{customer.tier}}'}, ctx with profile.attributes.tier='gold') returns 'gold' (reuses customerMerge + expandCustomerToken — shorthand customer.tier === customer.attributes.tier).
resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{customer.email}}'}, ctx) returns the reserved profile column value.
resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{event.amount}}'}, ctx with event={amount:19.99}) returns the value sourced from enrollment.state.event (NEW event.* namespace).

resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{event.items.0.sku}}'}, ctx with nested event payload) resolves a DEEP dotted path via the event resolver (eventMerge / resolveEventPath analogous to expandCustomerPath).

resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{event.missing}}'}, ctx) resolves SAFELY to '' (or a documented sentinel) — never throws, never leaves the literal '{{event.missing}}' as the written attribute.
resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{customer.unknownAttr}}'}, ctx with no such attribute) resolves SAFELY (empty), no crash.

resolveValueSpec for an event.* expression when ctx.event is undefined (manual/segment enrollment left no event on state) returns the safe-empty value — confirms the manual/segment paths resolve safely.

resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'pre {{customer.tier}} post'}, ctx) renders the surrounding literal text (string interpolation, not just whole-token substitution) — same engine as renderTemplateBody.
an unknown namespace token {{order.total}} (not customer.*/event.*) is left for safe-empty handling and never crashes.
testFiles.criterion: An update-profile (set_attribute) step writes the profile attribute with a LITERAL value AND with an event-sourced value ... an undefined path resolves safely. Verified ... with unit tests for the value resolver.

assertions: validateAutomationDefinition accepts a set_attribute action with key + a bare scalar value (BACKWARD-COMPAT: the legacy static value shape still validates as an implicit literal).
validateAutomationDefinition accepts {kind:'set_attribute', key:'tier', value:{kind:'literal', value:'gold'}, next}.
validateAutomationDefinition accepts {kind:'set_attribute', key:'last_amount', value:{kind:'expression', expression:'{{event.amount}}'}, next}.
validateAutomationDefinition THROWS when value is {kind:'expression'} with a missing/empty expression string.
validateAutomationDefinition THROWS when value is {kind:'literal'} but the literal field is absent (an explicit-spec object missing its payload) — distinguishes from a bare scalar value.
validateAutomationDefinition THROWS on an unknown value-spec kind (e.g. {kind:'sql'}).
validateAutomationDefinition still THROWS when set_attribute has no key (existing rule unchanged).
validation is PURE: no DB/AWS import in the module under test; the function only inspects the definition object.

actionSideEffect (core.ts) for a set_attribute now emits a side effect carrying the VALUE SPEC (literal|expression), not a pre-resolved value, so resolution happens at runner execution against the live profile + enrollment.state.
testFiles.criterion: Extend validateAutomationDefinition for the set_attribute value spec (literal vs expression) and keep it pure + unit-tested.

assertions: buildEnrollmentInsertWithState(ws, camp, profile, startNode, {event:{type, payload, event_id}}) produces an INSERT writing state = jsonb {event:{...}} and still ON CONFLICT (automation_id, profile_id) DO NOTHING (re-enrollment 'once' guard preserved).
buildEnrollmentInsertWithState binds workspace_id at $1 and THROWS on a falsy workspaceId (tenant-isolation guard parity with buildEnrollmentInsert).
the persisted state.event mirrors EventRow shape relevant for event.* resolution: at least payload (and type/event_id for provenance) — NOT a profile snapshot.

the legacy buildEnrollmentInsert (no state) is unchanged and still used by segment_entry / manual paths (state stays the column default '{}'), so a later set_attribute reading event.* gets an undefined event and resolves safely.

parseEventEnrollmentTrigger / the intent now carries the event payload through to the insert (the EnrollmentIntent or a sibling carries {event}) — assert the event-trigger path threads payload to buildEnrollmentInsertWithState while segment/manual intents carry none.
testFiles.criterion: PERSIST the trigger event payload onto automation_enrollments.state at enrollment time (event-trigger enrollment); manual/segment enrollment leave event undefined which must resolve safely.

assertions: REAL Postgres (DATABASE_URL); unique file-local workspace/automation/profile UUIDs; seed a workspace + profile + an active event-trigger automation whose graph is trigger(event)->set_attribute->exit.
Event-trigger enrollment persists the trigger event payload onto automation_enrollments.state.event (assert the column after enrollFromEvent / the wired enroll path).
Running the enrollment tick with a LITERAL set_attribute (value {kind:'literal', value:'gold'}) writes profiles.attributes.tier='gold' (jsonb_set) for the enrolled profile only.

Running a set_attribute with {kind:'expression', expression:'{{event.amount}}'} writes profiles.attributes.last_purchase_amount equal to the value taken FROM enrollment.state.event.amount (NOT the literal token).
An expression referencing an UNDEFINED event path ({{event.nope}}) writes a safe-empty value (documented) and does NOT crash the tick; the enrollment still advances to exit/completed.
WORKSPACE-SCOPED: a second workspace with an identically-named profile/attribute is NOT mutated (the UPDATE binds workspace_id=$1; assert the other tenant's attributes untouched).

IDEMPOTENT on retry: re-running the same enrollment tick (or replaying the sweep) yields the SAME attribute value — the event-sourced value re-resolves from the persisted enrollment.state, so a retry is a no-op write, not a divergent value. Assert attributes equal before/after the second run.

Cross-workspace safety: runEnrollment loads workspace_id FROM the enrollment row; assert no statement writes with a workspace_id other than the enrollment's (mirrors the existing 'write not scoped to the enrollment workspace' guard).
testFiles.criterion: An update-profile (set_attribute) step writes the profile attribute with a LITERAL value AND with an event-sourced value (e.g. attributes.last_purchase_amount = {{event.amount}} taken from the trigger event persisted on enrollment.state); the write is workspace-scoped + idempotent on retry; an undefined path resolves safely. Verified against REAL Postgres.

assertions: REAL Postgres; capability-gated request context (manage_content). Seed a workspace with a library email_template + a verified domain + a domain_senders row.

Attaching a library template to a automation SEND node clones it: a new email_templates row with kind='copy' and source_template_id = the library id (reuse cloneTemplate's INSERT ... SELECT shape) — assert the new row id != library id.
The clone copies the design + body AND the envelope columns (subject, sender_id, to_address, from_selected) — assert they match the source (so a configured library template yields a sendable copy).
Editing the copy (PUT the copy's subject/sender_id/to_address) does NOT change the library original — re-SELECT the library row and assert it's unchanged.
The send node's template_id now references the COPY (assert the automation definition's send-node template_id == the copy id after attach).
Cross-workspace REFUSAL (inv.2): attaching/cloning a template_id that belongs to ANOTHER workspace returns 404/refusal and creates no copy (scopedQuery on the source SELECT yields no row).

Cross-workspace sender_id REFUSAL: setting the copy's sender_id to a domain_senders id from another workspace is rejected via validateSenderId ('sender_id is not a sender in this workspace') — reuse the existing helper.
Saving the copy with a sender_id that IS in the workspace succeeds (validateSenderId returns {senderId}).
testFiles.criterion: A send node clones its template into an independently-editable copy (kind=copy); editing the copy does not touch the library original; the envelope persists on the copy. Cross-workspace template/sender ids are refused (inv.2).

assertions: REAL Postgres. Activation is updateAutomation/the new publish path setting status='active'; assert a automation whose send node's copy has sender_id=NULL is REFUSED with a 409 and a NODE-SPECIFIC message ('Choose who the email is from' + the offending node id/label).

Mirror sendBroadcast's ORDERED 409s per send node: sender_id missing -> 'Choose who the email is from…'; then to_address blank -> 'Set the To field…'; then subject blank -> 'Add a subject line…' — assert each surfaces in that priority and names the node.
A automation with MULTIPLE send nodes reports the FIRST incomplete node (or aggregates) — assert the message identifies WHICH node is missing what (surface which node/what is missing).
With all three set (real named sender — NO no-reply fallback) on every send node AND a verified sending_domains row present, activation SUCCEEDS and automations.status becomes 'active'.

VERIFIED-DOMAIN gate runs AFTER the per-node envelope checks (parity with sendBroadcast order): a automation with complete envelopes but NO verified domain is refused with the verified-domain message ('No verified sending domain…').
A automation with NO send nodes (only set_attribute/wait/exit) activates without any envelope gating (the gate only applies to send nodes).
Cross-workspace: the activate path scopes the automation + every referenced send-node copy by ctx.workspaceId (a copy/sender from another workspace can never satisfy the gate).
testFiles.criterion: Activating a automation with a send node missing From/To/Subject is refused with a clear, node-specific message; once all are set (real named sender, no no-reply fallback) + a verified domain exists, it activates. Verified against REAL Postgres.

assertions: REAL Postgres + MOCKED SES (an injected SesEmailClient capturing SendEmailInput — never real mail), mirroring action-send-through-dispatcher.integration.test.ts.

Seed a verified workspace, a profile with attributes.tier='gold' and email, and a automation send node referencing a COPY template with subject='Hi {{customer.email}} ({{customer.tier}})', to_address='{{customer.email}}', sender_id->a verified-domain sender, compiled_html containing {{customer.tier}}.
Run the runner tick: it inserts ONE outbox row with automation_id set + dedupe_key 'automation:<camp>:<profile>:<node>' and enqueues {outbox_id}; then run the REAL dispatchOutbox per id.

SES captured input: From == '"Name" <sender@verified-domain>' (resolved from sender_id — NO no-reply fallback used), To == the rendered customer.email, Subject has the merge tags RENDERED (the v0.27.2 subject fix — not the raw {{...}}), body has {{customer.tier}} rendered to 'gold'.
messages_log gets a row with automation_id = the automation (and broadcast_id NULL) and ses_message_id from the mock.
Guard pipeline holds: a SUPPRESSED recipient is SKIPPED (no SES call, no messages_log); the runner NEVER calls SES directly. An UNVERIFIED workspace is REFUSED at the gate.
Frequency-cap / quiet-hours short-circuit correctly when payload carries them (parity with existing dispatcher tests) — assert a capped recipient is skipped.
EXACTLY-ONCE: replaying the runner tick (same dedupe_key) inserts no second outbox row; replaying dispatchOutbox after a successful send re-claims nothing (one messages_log row total).
testFiles.criterion: A automation send flows through outbox -> Dispatcher -> messages_log(automation_id) with subject/To/body merge tags rendered; passes suppression/cap/quiet-hours. AWS/SES mocked.

instructions: Clear the turbo cache and RUN the tests yourself (DATABASE_URL set) twice from cold, plus pnpm typecheck and pnpm lint. For UI phases, RUN the Playwright e2e in a real browser. Record real pass/fail. Confirm the integration tier RUNS (real Postgres, not skipped/mocked) and no regression in earlier phases or the broadcast suite.

Verify EACH acceptance criterion is covered by a passing, non-vacuous test: ["A send node clones its template into an independently-editable copy (kind=copy); editing the copy does not touch the library original; the envelope persists on the copy. Cross-workspace template/sender ids are refused (inv.2).","Activating a automation with a send node missing From/To/Subject is refused with a clear, node-specific message; once all are set (real named sender, no no-reply fallback) + a verified domain exists, it activates. Verified against REAL Postgres.","A automation send flows through outbox → Dispatcher → messages_log(automation_id) with subject/To/body merge tags rendered; passes suppression/cap/quiet-hours. AWS/SES mocked.","An update-profile (set_attribute) step writes the profile attribute with a LITERAL value AND with an event-sourced value (e.g. attributes.last_purchase_amount = {{event.amount}} taken from the trigger event persisted on enrollment.state); the write is workspace-scoped + idempotent on retry; an undefined path resolves safely. Verified against REAL Postgres with unit tests for the value resolver."].

Highest-priority checks: (a) tenant isolation + workspace_id never client-supplied (server-side); (b) all automation sends route through the Dispatcher gating with the mandatory From/To/Subject envelope (no no-reply); (c) runner idempotency/concurrency (no double-advance / double-send / double-webhook); (d) webhook SSRF + domain allowlist + injected HTTP (no real host) + bounded retries/timeout; (e) workspace-tz time math is DST-correct; (f) the DSL stays the single graph model (no loops/orphans/stored coords); (g) for UI, the flow works in a REAL browser, server buttons spinner+lock, NO native dialogs, data-testid preserved.
If any integration test mocks Postgres, that is a FAIL. If a UI criterion is only unit-tested (no real browser), that is a FAIL. If the webhook test hits a real host, that is a FAIL.
Scope check: flag any feature beyond the agreed design. Confirm the root version was bumped + CLAUDE.md updated when behavior changed.
Score 0-100. Set testsPass and criteriaAllMet honestly. Give concrete prioritized recommendations.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { score: number, testsPass: boolean, criteriaAllMet: boolean, criteriaCoverage: [{criterion, met, evidence}], isolationOk: boolean, idempotencyOk: boolean, webhookSafetyOk: boolean, browserVerified: boolean, scopeCreep: string[], recommendations: string[], criticalIssues: string[] }
prompt.role: adversarial principal reviewer + QA engineer verifying spec-fidelity, tenant isolation, runner idempotency, webhook safety, and (for UI) real-browser behavior
prompt.task: Independently verify "Action nodes: send-email instance + update-profile (event-sourced)" against its criteria. Re-run the tests yourself (cold cache); do not trust the self-report.

title: Quality gate: Action nodes: send-email instance + update-profile (event-sourced) (iteration 1)

commandsRun: pnpm --filter @cdp/shared exec vitest run test/expression-value.test.ts (20 passed)
pnpm --filter @cdp/service-automation-runner exec vitest run test/set-attribute-value-spec.test.ts (10 passed)
pnpm --filter @cdp/service-automation-runner exec vitest run test/enrollment-event-state.test.ts (5 passed)
DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp pnpm --filter @cdp/service-automation-runner exec vitest run test/set-attribute-event-sourced.integration.test.ts (2 passed)
DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp pnpm --filter @cdp/service-automation-runner exec vitest run test/automation-send-dispatcher-instance.integration.test.ts (1 passed)
DATABASE_URL=... pnpm --filter @cdp/service-local-api exec vitest run test/automation-send-node-clone.integration.test.ts test/automation-publish-gate.integration.test.ts (10 passed)
pnpm typecheck (43/43 successful)

DATABASE_URL=... pnpm --filter @cdp/shared --filter @cdp/service-automation-runner --filter @cdp/service-local-api --filter @cdp/service-dispatcher exec vitest run (cold: 55 + 165 + 189 + 71 = 480 passed)
pnpm lint (all touched backend packages clean; only the 7 KNOWN pre-existing web react-hooks/exhaustive-deps 'rule not found' errors in untouched files remain)
pnpm build (24/24 successful)
impl.e2eSummary: No e2e — backend-only phase (phase.frontend=false; no builder UI, phases 5/6).
impl.notes: PART A (send-node instance): POST /automations/:id/send-nodes/:nodeId/attach-template clones a library template into a kind='copy' working copy (source_template_id; copies design + subject/sender_id/to_address/from_selected via the same INSERT...SELECT as cloneTemplate) and repoints the send node's template_id at the copy; source SELECT scoped to ctx.workspaceId (cross-workspace 404, inv.2). POST /automations/:id/activate adds publish gating that did not exist before: pure collectSendNodeEnvelopeGaps (automation-runner dsl.ts, BFS order) feeds sendBroadcast-ordered per-node 409s (sender_id -> to_address -> subject, naming the node) then the verified sending_domains gate (only when >=1 send node; a no-send automation activates ungated). Dispatcher UNCHANGED — a automation outbox row's template_id is the copy, so dispatch.ts resolves sender_id->From + renders subject/To/body exactly as for broadcasts (verified end-to-end via the integration test: messages_log.automation_id set, broadcast_id null, exactly-once on replay). PART B (event-sourced set_attribute): new @cdp/shared event.ts (event.* namespace twin of customer.ts) + expression.ts (ValueSpec, renderExpression shared {{token}} engine, resolveValueSpec with literal | expression | legacy-bare-scalar + safe-empty). validateAutomationDefinition extended with validateSetAttributeValue (pure). buildEnrollmentInsertWithState persists {event:{type,payload,event_id}} onto automation_enrollments.state ONLY in enrollFromEvent; the other three enrollment paths keep the plain insert so state.event is undefined and an event.* expression resolves safe-empty. run.ts loads the profile + state.event.payload under the FOR UPDATE lock and resolves the value spec before the existing parameterized buildSetAttribute (workspace_id at $1) — read-only substitution, never SQL (inv.6 untouched); idempotent because the source state is immutable. NO migration (automation_enrollments.state jsonb DEFAULT '{}' already exists, migration 0005). NO builder UI (phases 5/6). Not pushed.
impl.testSummary: All affected backend suites GREEN on a cold run: @cdp/shared 55/55 (4 files), @cdp/service-dispatcher 71/71 (12 files), @cdp/service-automation-runner 165/165 (31 files), @cdp/service-local-api 189/189 (43 files). New phase-4 tests: expression-value (20 unit, value resolver literal/customer.*/event.*/deep-dot/undefined-safe), set-attribute-value-spec (10 unit, validateSetAttributeValue + side-effect carries the spec), enrollment-event-state (5 unit, buildEnrollmentInsertWithState + intent threads the event), set-attribute-event-sourced.integration (2, real Postgres: state.event persisted, literal + {{event.amount}} written, undefined path safe-empty, workspace-scoped, idempotent retry), automation-send-dispatcher-instance.integration (1, runner->real dispatcher: From from sender_id, subject/To/body merge rendered, messages_log automation_id, exactly-once), automation-send-node-clone.integration (3, clone kind=copy + envelope copy + cross-ws refusal), automation-publish-gate.integration (7, ordered per-node 409s + verified-domain gate + no-send ungated + cross-ws 404). Updated parse-event-trigger.test.ts for the new intent.event field. No mock of Postgres in the integration tier; SES mocked via an injected SesEmailClient.

criteria: A send node clones its template into an independently-editable copy (kind=copy); editing the copy does not touch the library original; the envelope persists on the copy. Cross-workspace template/sender ids are refused (inv.2).

Activating a automation with a send node missing From/To/Subject is refused with a clear, node-specific message; once all are set (real named sender, no no-reply fallback) + a verified domain exists, it activates. Verified against REAL Postgres.
A automation send flows through outbox → Dispatcher → messages_log(automation_id) with subject/To/body merge tags rendered; passes suppression/cap/quiet-hours. AWS/SES mocked.

An update-profile (set_attribute) step writes the profile attribute with a LITERAL value AND with an event-sourced value (e.g. attributes.last_purchase_amount = {{event.amount}} taken from the trigger event persisted on enrollment.state); the write is workspace-scoped + idempotent on retry; an undefined path resolves safely. Verified against REAL Postgres with unit tests for the value resolver.
phase.refs: CLAUDE.md (broadcast email-INSTANCE/clone model, sendBroadcast gating, dispatcher automation path, customer.* merge) + §9B action(send|set_attribute)
scope: Give each SEND node its OWN editable email instance, reusing the broadcast flow: attaching a template CLONES it (kind=copy, source_template_id) into the node's working copy; the envelope (real named From sender + To token + Subject) lives on that copy and is edited in the email editor (autosaved). The send node references the copy template_id.

PUBLISH-time gating: a automation cannot be activated if any send node lacks a sendable email (From sender + To + Subject all set) — mirror sendBroadcast's ordered 409s + the verified-domain gate. Surface which node/what is missing.

Verify the Dispatcher automation path end-to-end: automation outbox row → Dispatcher resolves sender_id → renders subject/To/body merge tags (the v0.27.2 subject fix) → messages_log with automation_id. No change to the send pipeline beyond what the instance model needs.

UPDATE-PROFILE action (the set_attribute action kind already exists + the runner already applies a STATIC value via buildSetAttribute): extend it so the value can be a LITERAL **or** an EXPRESSION sourced from the trigger event or the profile — e.g. customer.* and a new event.* namespace ({{event.<payload path>}}). To enable event.*, PERSIST the trigger event payload onto automation_enrollments.state at enrollment time (event-trigger enrollment from phase 3) so a later update-profile step can read it; resolve the expression at execution against the profile + enrollment.state.event (reuse the @cdp/shared customer.* resolver; add an analogous event.* resolver). A missing/again-undefined path resolves safely (no crash; documented — skip or empty).

Extend validateAutomationDefinition for the set_attribute value spec (literal vs expression) and keep it pure + unit-tested. The runner write stays workspace-scoped + idempotent (re-applying the same set_attribute on a retried tick is naturally idempotent for a literal; for an event-sourced value it resolves from the persisted enrollment.state, so a retry yields the same write).
phase.title: Action nodes: send-email instance + update-profile (event-sourced)

implUnits: 1. @cdp/shared: add the event.* namespace resolver — EVENT_PREFIX, resolveEventPath, eventMerge, expandEventToken — modeled on customer.ts; export from the package index. (No DB; pure.)

2. @cdp/shared: extract renderExpression (the {{token}} interpolation) so the dispatcher's renderTemplateBody and the new value resolver share one substitution engine; define resolveValueSpec(spec, ctx) with literal | expression | legacy-bare-scalar handling and safe-empty undefined resolution.

3. automation-runner dsl.ts: define the ValueSpec type ({kind:'literal', value} | {kind:'expression', expression}); extend ActionNode.value typing; add validateSetAttributeValue and call it from validateNodeFields' set_attribute branch (accept legacy bare scalar for back-compat).

4. automation-runner core.ts: change the set_attribute SideEffect to carry the VALUE SPEC (not a pre-resolved value); update actionSideEffect accordingly. Keep buildSetAttribute unchanged (it receives the already-resolved value).
5. automation-runner core.ts: add buildEnrollmentInsertWithState (persists state jsonb) alongside buildEnrollmentInsert; thread the trigger event payload through the event-enrollment intent.

6. automation-runner enroll.ts: in enrollFromEvent, persist {event:{type,payload,event_id}} onto enrollment.state via buildEnrollmentInsertWithState; segment_entry/manual paths keep buildEnrollmentInsert (state stays default '{}').

7. automation-runner run.ts: in the FOR-UPDATE tick load enrollment.state (already part of the row read — add state to the SELECT) and the profile; when applying a set_attribute side effect, call resolveValueSpec({profile, event: state.event}) THEN buildSetAttribute(ws, profile, key, resolvedValue). Resolution is read-only against the locked row + post-loaded profile, so a retry re-resolves identically (idempotent).

8. local-api handlers.ts: add a automation send-node CLONE path (reuse cloneTemplate's INSERT...SELECT copying design+envelope columns; kind='copy', source_template_id) and point the send node's template_id at the copy; scope the source SELECT by ctx.workspaceId (cross-workspace refusal).

9. local-api handlers.ts: add publish-time gating to the activate path (updateAutomation setting status='active', or a dedicated POST /automations/:id/activate) — for each send node, load its copy's (sender_id,to_address,subject) and emit sendBroadcast-ordered 409s naming the node; then the verified-domain gate. Reuse validateSenderId for any sender writes.
10. local-api routes.ts: register any new route(s) under the manage_content capability (clone/attach) consistent with existing automation routes; activation stays manage_content.

11. Process hygiene: bump root package.json (minor — feature); update CLAUDE.md (event.* namespace, set_attribute value spec, enrollment.state.event persistence, automation send-node instance + publish gate); NO new migration (automation_enrollments.state already exists). Rebuild changed packages (tsc -b) before restarting dev:api; apply nothing to the DB schema.
mocks: SES: an injected SesEmailClient capturing SendEmailInput (aws-sdk-client-mock style / a hand fake) — NEVER sends real mail; assert From/To/Subject/body and ConfigurationSet.
SQS: the injected SqsSender (SendMessageCommand) is a fake capturing enqueued {outbox_id} bodies (the runner boundary).
Clock: deps.now() injected for deterministic cap/quiet-hours/wait math.
No Postgres mock in the integration tier — REAL local Postgres (DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/cdp); pure tiers use in-memory objects only.
Webhook HTTP client: not exercised this phase (no webhook nodes), but unchanged injection contract preserved.
plan.notes: automation_enrollments.state jsonb NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}' ALREADY EXISTS (packages/db/supabase/migrations/0005_messaging.sql) — CONFIRMED, NO migration needed to persist the trigger event there. Today buildEnrollmentInsert (core.ts) does NOT write state, so all current enrollment paths leave it at the default '{}'. The trigger event is in hand ONLY in enrollFromEvent (phase 3, EventRow with payload/type/event_id); that is the single place to persist state.event (via a new buildEnrollmentInsertWithState). The OTHER enrollment paths (enrollFromSegmentChange, enrollProfileManually, enrollSegmentSnapshot) have NO event, so they keep the plain buildEnrollmentInsert and state.event stays UNDEFINED — therefore a set_attribute with an event.* expression on a segment/manual-enrolled profile MUST resolve safely to empty (no crash): this is a required, explicitly-tested behavior, not an edge case. Activation today is just updateAutomation setting status='active' (no publish gating exists yet); the new gate mirrors sendBroadcast's ordered 409s (sender_id -> to_address -> subject) PER SEND NODE, then the verified-domain gate, naming the offending node. The dispatcher already renders subject/To/body merge tags and resolves sender_id->From with NO no-reply fallback reachable for a configured instance, so the automation send path needs no dispatcher change beyond the instance model the broadcast flow established. Idempotency for the event-sourced write is structural: the value re-resolves from the IMMUTABLE persisted enrollment.state under the FOR UPDATE lock, so a retried tick produces the identical jsonb_set write (a literal is trivially idempotent; an expression is idempotent because its source — state.event — never changes). All resolution is read-only string substitution (never interpolated into SQL), so invariant 6 is untouched; the only SQL is the existing parameterized buildSetAttribute with workspace_id at $1.
pureFunctions: @cdp/shared resolveEventPath(payload, pathAfterEvent): deep-dot resolver into the event payload (analogous to expandCustomerPath) — returns the leaf value or undefined.

@cdp/shared eventMerge(event): build the merge map for the 'event.*' namespace from a persisted trigger event payload (FULL tokens 'event.<path>' -> stringified scalars; nested objects/arrays skipped for whole-tag substitution, but dotted leaf paths supported via resolveEventPath). Pairs with the renderer like customerMerge.
@cdp/shared expandEventToken(token) / EVENT_PREFIX: normalize an 'event.*' token (no shorthand to expand, but keep symmetry with expandCustomerToken).

resolveValueSpec(spec, ctx:{profile, event}): the value resolver — a literal returns spec.value (or a bare scalar for back-compat); an expression renders via a shared interpolation over {customer.*, event.*} merge maps; an undefined/unknown path resolves to a documented safe-empty value; never throws. Lives in @cdp/shared (used by the runner; symmetrical with renderTemplateBody).

renderExpression(template, merge): the shared {{token}} interpolation engine extracted from dispatcher renderTemplateBody so the value resolver and the dispatcher share ONE substitution rule (unknown tags safe-empty for value resolution; same expandCustomerToken normalization).
isExpressionSpec(value) / isLiteralSpec(value) / coerceLegacyLiteral(value): pure value-spec discriminators used by both validateAutomationDefinition and resolveValueSpec.
validateSetAttributeValue(id, value): pure structural validation of the set_attribute value spec (literal vs expression vs legacy bare scalar), invoked from validateAutomationDefinition.

buildEnrollmentInsertWithState(workspaceId, automationId, profileId, startNode, state): SqlStatement that persists automation_enrollments.state (e.g. {event:{...}}) on insert; ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING preserved; workspace_id at $1; THROWS on falsy workspaceId.
buildSetAttribute (existing, core.ts) reused verbatim once the value is resolved — it already does workspace-scoped jsonb_set; resolution happens BEFORE building this statement.

collectSendNodeEnvelopeGaps(definition, copiesById): pure helper that walks send nodes and yields per-node {nodeId, missing:['sender'|'to'|'subject']} in sendBroadcast order — feeds the publish-gate message.
reuse: @cdp/shared customer.ts (customerMerge, expandCustomerToken, expandCustomerPath, resolveCustomerField) — the event.* resolver is a structural twin; renderExpression is extracted from dispatcher renderTemplateBody so both share ONE {{token}} engine.

dispatcher core.ts renderTemplateBody / buildSendEmailInput — already render subject/To/body merge tags (the v0.27.2 subject fix); the automation send path needs NO dispatcher change beyond the instance model already supporting sender_id/to_address/subject on the template.
automation-runner core.ts buildAutomationOutboxInsert / buildAutomationDedupeKey / buildSetAttribute / buildEnrollmentInsert — reuse verbatim; add buildEnrollmentInsertWithState as a sibling.

automation-runner run.ts chainTick + runEnrollmentInTx FOR-UPDATE lock + advanceFor — the set_attribute resolution slots into the existing side-effect application loop; idempotency/concurrency guarantees are preserved unchanged.

automation-runner enroll.ts enrollFromEvent (phase 3) — the only enrollment path that has the trigger event in hand; it is where state.event is persisted. enrollFromSegmentChange / enrollProfileManually / enrollSegmentSnapshot intentionally leave event undefined.

local-api handlers.ts cloneTemplate (INSERT...SELECT copying design + subject/sender_id/to_address/from_selected), validateSenderId (cross-workspace sender refusal, inv.2), sendBroadcast's ordered 409 messages + verified-domain gate — mirror these verbatim for the automation send-node clone + publish gate.

Existing test scaffolding: action-send-through-dispatcher.integration.test.ts (runner->real dispatcher with mocked SES, verified/unverified/suppressed cases), packages/shared/test/customer.test.ts (resolver unit-test style), services/local-api/test/broadcast-send-gate.integration.test.ts (ordered 409 gate assertions), automation-enroll-endpoint.integration.test.ts (enrollment scoping).

@cdp/segments AstNode + evaluateEventPayloadFilter (already in core.ts) — the event payload is already trusted/closed-grammar at enroll time; the value resolver reads the SAME persisted payload, no new whitelist needed for resolution (it's read-only substitution, never SQL).

assertions: resolveValueSpec({kind:'literal', value:42}, ctx) returns 42 unchanged (number preserved, not stringified).
resolveValueSpec({kind:'literal', value:'gold'}, ctx) returns 'gold'.
resolveValueSpec({kind:'literal', value:null}, ctx) returns null (an explicit null literal is honored, distinct from undefined).

resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{customer.tier}}'}, ctx with profile.attributes.tier='gold') returns 'gold' (reuses customerMerge + expandCustomerToken — shorthand customer.tier === customer.attributes.tier).
resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{customer.email}}'}, ctx) returns the reserved profile column value.
resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{event.amount}}'}, ctx with event={amount:19.99}) returns the value sourced from enrollment.state.event (NEW event.* namespace).

resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{event.items.0.sku}}'}, ctx with nested event payload) resolves a DEEP dotted path via the event resolver (eventMerge / resolveEventPath analogous to expandCustomerPath).

resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{event.missing}}'}, ctx) resolves SAFELY to '' (or a documented sentinel) — never throws, never leaves the literal '{{event.missing}}' as the written attribute.
resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'{{customer.unknownAttr}}'}, ctx with no such attribute) resolves SAFELY (empty), no crash.

resolveValueSpec for an event.* expression when ctx.event is undefined (manual/segment enrollment left no event on state) returns the safe-empty value — confirms the manual/segment paths resolve safely.

resolveValueSpec({kind:'expression', expression:'pre {{customer.tier}} post'}, ctx) renders the surrounding literal text (string interpolation, not just whole-token substitution) — same engine as renderTemplateBody.
an unknown namespace token {{order.total}} (not customer.*/event.*) is left for safe-empty handling and never crashes.
testFiles.criterion: An update-profile (set_attribute) step writes the profile attribute with a LITERAL value AND with an event-sourced value ... an undefined path resolves safely. Verified ... with unit tests for the value resolver.

assertions: validateAutomationDefinition accepts a set_attribute action with key + a bare scalar value (BACKWARD-COMPAT: the legacy static value shape still validates as an implicit literal).
validateAutomationDefinition accepts {kind:'set_attribute', key:'tier', value:{kind:'literal', value:'gold'}, next}.
validateAutomationDefinition accepts {kind:'set_attribute', key:'last_amount', value:{kind:'expression', expression:'{{event.amount}}'}, next}.
validateAutomationDefinition THROWS when value is {kind:'expression'} with a missing/empty expression string.
validateAutomationDefinition THROWS when value is {kind:'literal'} but the literal field is absent (an explicit-spec object missing its payload) — distinguishes from a bare scalar value.
validateAutomationDefinition THROWS on an unknown value-spec kind (e.g. {kind:'sql'}).
validateAutomationDefinition still THROWS when set_attribute has no key (existing rule unchanged).
validation is PURE: no DB/AWS import in the module under test; the function only inspects the definition object.

actionSideEffect (core.ts) for a set_attribute now emits a side effect carrying the VALUE SPEC (literal|expression), not a pre-resolved value, so resolution happens at runner execution against the live profile + enrollment.state.
testFiles.criterion: Extend validateAutomationDefinition for the set_attribute value spec (literal vs expression) and keep it pure + unit-tested.

assertions: buildEnrollmentInsertWithState(ws, camp, profile, startNode, {event:{type, payload, event_id}}) produces an INSERT writing state = jsonb {event:{...}} and still ON CONFLICT (automation_id, profile_id) DO NOTHING (re-enrollment 'once' guard preserved).
buildEnrollmentInsertWithState binds workspace_id at $1 and THROWS on a falsy workspaceId (tenant-isolation guard parity with buildEnrollmentInsert).
the persisted state.event mirrors EventRow shape relevant for event.* resolution: at least payload (and type/event_id for provenance) — NOT a profile snapshot.

the legacy buildEnrollmentInsert (no state) is unchanged and still used by segment_entry / manual paths (state stays the column default '{}'), so a later set_attribute reading event.* gets an undefined event and resolves safely.

parseEventEnrollmentTrigger / the intent now carries the event payload through to the insert (the EnrollmentIntent or a sibling carries {event}) — assert the event-trigger path threads payload to buildEnrollmentInsertWithState while segment/manual intents carry none.
testFiles.criterion: PERSIST the trigger event payload onto automation_enrollments.state at enrollment time (event-trigger enrollment); manual/segment enrollment leave event undefined which must resolve safely.

assertions: REAL Postgres (DATABASE_URL); unique file-local workspace/automation/profile UUIDs; seed a workspace + profile + an active event-trigger automation whose graph is trigger(event)->set_attribute->exit.
Event-trigger enrollment persists the trigger event payload onto automation_enrollments.state.event (assert the column after enrollFromEvent / the wired enroll path).
Running the enrollment tick with a LITERAL set_attribute (value {kind:'literal', value:'gold'}) writes profiles.attributes.tier='gold' (jsonb_set) for the enrolled profile only.

Running a set_attribute with {kind:'expression', expression:'{{event.amount}}'} writes profiles.attributes.last_purchase_amount equal to the value taken FROM enrollment.state.event.amount (NOT the literal token).
An expression referencing an UNDEFINED event path ({{event.nope}}) writes a safe-empty value (documented) and does NOT crash the tick; the enrollment still advances to exit/completed.
WORKSPACE-SCOPED: a second workspace with an identically-named profile/attribute is NOT mutated (the UPDATE binds workspace_id=$1; assert the other tenant's attributes untouched).

IDEMPOTENT on retry: re-running the same enrollment tick (or replaying the sweep) yields the SAME attribute value — the event-sourced value re-resolves from the persisted enrollment.state, so a retry is a no-op write, not a divergent value. Assert attributes equal before/after the second run.

Cross-workspace safety: runEnrollment loads workspace_id FROM the enrollment row; assert no statement writes with a workspace_id other than the enrollment's (mirrors the existing 'write not scoped to the enrollment workspace' guard).
testFiles.criterion: An update-profile (set_attribute) step writes the profile attribute with a LITERAL value AND with an event-sourced value (e.g. attributes.last_purchase_amount = {{event.amount}} taken from the trigger event persisted on enrollment.state); the write is workspace-scoped + idempotent on retry; an undefined path resolves safely. Verified against REAL Postgres.

assertions: REAL Postgres; capability-gated request context (manage_content). Seed a workspace with a library email_template + a verified domain + a domain_senders row.

Attaching a library template to a automation SEND node clones it: a new email_templates row with kind='copy' and source_template_id = the library id (reuse cloneTemplate's INSERT ... SELECT shape) — assert the new row id != library id.
The clone copies the design + body AND the envelope columns (subject, sender_id, to_address, from_selected) — assert they match the source (so a configured library template yields a sendable copy).
Editing the copy (PUT the copy's subject/sender_id/to_address) does NOT change the library original — re-SELECT the library row and assert it's unchanged.
The send node's template_id now references the COPY (assert the automation definition's send-node template_id == the copy id after attach).
Cross-workspace REFUSAL (inv.2): attaching/cloning a template_id that belongs to ANOTHER workspace returns 404/refusal and creates no copy (scopedQuery on the source SELECT yields no row).

Cross-workspace sender_id REFUSAL: setting the copy's sender_id to a domain_senders id from another workspace is rejected via validateSenderId ('sender_id is not a sender in this workspace') — reuse the existing helper.
Saving the copy with a sender_id that IS in the workspace succeeds (validateSenderId returns {senderId}).
testFiles.criterion: A send node clones its template into an independently-editable copy (kind=copy); editing the copy does not touch the library original; the envelope persists on the copy. Cross-workspace template/sender ids are refused (inv.2).

assertions: REAL Postgres. Activation is updateAutomation/the new publish path setting status='active'; assert a automation whose send node's copy has sender_id=NULL is REFUSED with a 409 and a NODE-SPECIFIC message ('Choose who the email is from' + the offending node id/label).

Mirror sendBroadcast's ORDERED 409s per send node: sender_id missing -> 'Choose who the email is from…'; then to_address blank -> 'Set the To field…'; then subject blank -> 'Add a subject line…' — assert each surfaces in that priority and names the node.
A automation with MULTIPLE send nodes reports the FIRST incomplete node (or aggregates) — assert the message identifies WHICH node is missing what (surface which node/what is missing).
With all three set (real named sender — NO no-reply fallback) on every send node AND a verified sending_domains row present, activation SUCCEEDS and automations.status becomes 'active'.

VERIFIED-DOMAIN gate runs AFTER the per-node envelope checks (parity with sendBroadcast order): a automation with complete envelopes but NO verified domain is refused with the verified-domain message ('No verified sending domain…').
A automation with NO send nodes (only set_attribute/wait/exit) activates without any envelope gating (the gate only applies to send nodes).
Cross-workspace: the activate path scopes the automation + every referenced send-node copy by ctx.workspaceId (a copy/sender from another workspace can never satisfy the gate).
testFiles.criterion: Activating a automation with a send node missing From/To/Subject is refused with a clear, node-specific message; once all are set (real named sender, no no-reply fallback) + a verified domain exists, it activates. Verified against REAL Postgres.

assertions: REAL Postgres + MOCKED SES (an injected SesEmailClient capturing SendEmailInput — never real mail), mirroring action-send-through-dispatcher.integration.test.ts.

Seed a verified workspace, a profile with attributes.tier='gold' and email, and a automation send node referencing a COPY template with subject='Hi {{customer.email}} ({{customer.tier}})', to_address='{{customer.email}}', sender_id->a verified-domain sender, compiled_html containing {{customer.tier}}.
Run the runner tick: it inserts ONE outbox row with automation_id set + dedupe_key 'automation:<camp>:<profile>:<node>' and enqueues {outbox_id}; then run the REAL dispatchOutbox per id.

SES captured input: From == '"Name" <sender@verified-domain>' (resolved from sender_id — NO no-reply fallback used), To == the rendered customer.email, Subject has the merge tags RENDERED (the v0.27.2 subject fix — not the raw {{...}}), body has {{customer.tier}} rendered to 'gold'.
messages_log gets a row with automation_id = the automation (and broadcast_id NULL) and ses_message_id from the mock.
Guard pipeline holds: a SUPPRESSED recipient is SKIPPED (no SES call, no messages_log); the runner NEVER calls SES directly. An UNVERIFIED workspace is REFUSED at the gate.
Frequency-cap / quiet-hours short-circuit correctly when payload carries them (parity with existing dispatcher tests) — assert a capped recipient is skipped.
EXACTLY-ONCE: replaying the runner tick (same dedupe_key) inserts no second outbox row; replaying dispatchOutbox after a successful send re-claims nothing (one messages_log row total).
testFiles.criterion: A automation send flows through outbox -> Dispatcher -> messages_log(automation_id) with subject/To/body merge tags rendered; passes suppression/cap/quiet-hours. AWS/SES mocked.
